title: cheilocostus candidus
intro: Cheilocostus candidus, also known as the White Costus, is a species of flowering plant in the Costaceae family. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian and Pacific Island medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant's health benefits include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels. Cheilocostus candidus can be prepared into herbal remedies in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various ailments.
